chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD 1. C.P. No.D- 1183 of 2018 2. C.P. No.D- 2302 of 2019 3. C.P. No.D- 2716 of 2019 4. C.P. No.D- 154 of 2020 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 22.04.2021 Mr. Wali Muhammad Khoso, Advocate for petitioner in C.P. No.D- 1183. Mr. Nasrullah Korai Advocate for petitioner in C.P. No.D- 2302 of 2019. Petitioner Allah Bachayo Jatoi is present on interim bail in C.P. No.D- 2302 of 2019. Mr. Bashir Ahmed Almani, Advocate for petitioners in C.P. No.D- 2716 of 2019 and 154 of 2020. Mr. Jangu Khan, Special Prosecutor NAB. = This a bunch of four Constitutional Petitions, filed by the petitioners with regard to same reference and the impugned judgment. However, since Constitution Petition No.D-2302 of 2019 has been filed by petitioner Allah Bachayo Jatoi for pre-arrest bail; whereas, the other matters are with regard to post-arrest bail in the same subject reference being No.02 of 2018, therefore, the petition bearing No.D-2302 of 2019 is hereby de-tagged from the other petitions. Let it be fixed separately on 27.05.2021. At this juncture, Ms. Razia Ali Zaman Patoli Advocate has appeared and submits that C.P. No.D- 1472/2020 and Cr. Acquittal Appeal No.D-04/2020, which have also been filed in respect of the same subject reference as well as the impugned judgment, are not fixed alongwith the present petitions. She requests that both the aforementioned petition as well as the appeal may be connected and fixed alongwith C.P. No.D- 2302 of 2019 on the next date of hearing. Order accordingly. As regards, the petitions being Nos.D-1183/2018, 2716/2019 and 154/2020 are concerned, learned Special Prosecutor for NAB submits that

Order accordingly. As regards, the petitions being Nos.D-1183/2018, 2716/2019 and 154/2020 are concerned, learned Special Prosecutor for NAB submits that these petitions may be disposed of in the same way as of other matters wherein this Court disposed of those petitions with direction to the trial Court to hear the cases preferably on day-to-day basis, which order has been complied with and the matters are being proceeded swiftly. Similar request is made in these matters also. In the given circumstances, the trial Court is directed to proceed with these matters preferably on day-to-day basis also and pass a judgment in accordance with law as expeditiously as possible within a period of one month. Constitutional petitions Nos.D-1183/2018, 2716/2019 and 154/2020 are disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE S
